div.head: The Ambassador in Great Britain (Page) to the Secretary of State
div.dateline:
American Embassy,
London, December 22, 1914, 7 p.m.
[Received December 23, 8 a.m.]
div.p:
1319. Your 776, December 18.Not printed. Sir Edward Grey informs me that his understanding is that Italy does not prohibit export of cotton, unless it be perhaps because of consignment to order, and that the Italian Government knows that Great Britain will stop no ship because of cotton and no cargo of cotton. When attention was called to the statement that American cotton was stopped while
Egyptian was permitted to go, he denied its accuracy, and said that he had had an Egyptian complaint that Egyptian cotton was stopped and American was permitted to go, which also he denied.
